 The normal lab results and absence of substance use rule out other potential causes.
 The mental status examination shows significant impairment in mood, affect, and thought content, reinforcing the diagnosis.
 The comprehensive assessment should focus on the impact of these symptoms on her daily life, particularly her ability to function at work and maintain social relationships.
 A treatment plan addressing both depressive and psychotic symptoms is crucial to improve her functioning and quality of life.
